meta.canonical_note: DB is source of truth post-M34 for programme-stage narrative (L2 of 3-layer reference).
meta.description: Programme-wide narrative section index — anchor verse definition, XREF architecture, validation standard, etc. Content populated via PROSE patches as researcher + AI draft each narrative.

types.label: Programme — Anchor Verse Definition
types.description: Canonical definition of anchor verses, dual purpose (analytical anchor + verse-context seed), minimum requirements. Authoritative home for the concept (currently mirrored in wa-reference §16 and wa-dimensionreview-instruction §4.2).

types.label: Programme — XREF Architecture
types.description: OWNER / XREF term semantics, verse_context inheritance, canonical-row rule, cross-registry link pattern (currently scattered across wa-reference §17, wa-registry-management-guide).

types.label: Programme — Document Validation Standard
types.description: Inflection point completeness; gap status discipline; cross-doc consistency; dry-run gate assessment (currently wa-reference §18).

types.label: Programme — Soft-Delete Discipline
types.description: Delete_flagged semantics; cascade rules; no physical deletes in automated flows; audit-trail retention.

types.label: Programme — Field Authority Rules
types.description: mti_term_flags canonical for somatic / god_as_subject; deprecation notes; which field wins on conflict.

types.label: Programme — Backup Discipline
types.description: Per-migration backup; per-patch backup; retention period; restoration procedure.

types.label: Programme — Patch Failure / REPAIR Protocol
types.description: What to do when a patch is rejected; failure patches; mid-pool recovery.

types.label: Programme — Instruction Override Protocol
types.description: How instruction overrides are declared, logged in observations, recorded in handoff documents.
